title: Paspalum nicorae
taxonomy: Paspalum nicorae is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a member of the genus Paspalum, which consists of various species of grasses native to different parts of the world. The genus Paspalum is further categorized under the subfamily Panicoideae, which includes many types of grasses. Paspalum nicorae is a part of the tribe Paniceae, a group of grasses characterized by their inflorescence structure. Within the tribe Paniceae, Paspalum nicorae is classified as a distinct species due to its unique morphological features.
uses: Paspalum nicorae has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Andean region.
